    • Advanced count time-series uncertainty and forecast evaluation

Simulate renewal-based count forecasts with uncertainty propagation

Source: R/forecast_uncertainty.R
simulate_renewal_forecast.Rd

Generates stochastic forecast trajectories from a renewal equation while propagating uncertainty in reproduction numbers (rt) and process noise. Future incidence is simulated recursively, so uncertainty compounds over the full forecast horizon.

Usage

simulate_renewal_forecast(
  incidence,
  serial_interval,
  rt = 1,
  horizon = NULL,
  n_sims = 1000L,
  seed = NULL
)

Arguments

incidence

Numeric vector of historical incidence counts.

serial_interval

Numeric serial interval probability vector where the first element corresponds to a lag of one time step.

rt

Reproduction-number input. Can be:

  • a scalar (constant across all simulations and forecast days),

  • a vector of length horizon (time-varying but shared across simulations), or

  • a matrix with n_sims rows and horizon columns (fully stochastic paths).

horizon

Number of forecast days. If NULL, inferred from rt when rt is a vector or matrix. Defaults to 7 when rt is scalar.

n_sims

Number of simulation paths when rt is scalar or vector.

seed

Optional random seed.

Value

A tibble with one row per simulation/day containing sim, day, rt, infectiousness, expected_incidence, and simulated incidence.
